visa_free.notes: Citizens of EU member states do not need a visa for entering Austria. Freedom of movement applies. Includes Croatia (EU/Schengen member since 2023).
EEA citizens do not need a visa for entering Austria. Freedom of movement applies.
Swiss nationals do not need a visa for entering Austria. Freedom of movement applies.

eta.nationality: All visa-exempt third-country nationals (forthcoming ETIAS requirement)
eta.notes: ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorization System) will be required for all currently visa-free third-country nationals entering the Schengen area. As of June 2026, ETIAS is NOT yet operational. The current official EU timeline targets Q4 2026 for launch. No ETIAS applications are being accepted yet. Once operational, it will function as a pre-travel authorisation (not a visa) for stays up to 90 days.

visa_policy.notes: Austria is a Schengen member; visa policy is governed by EU Regulation 2018/1806. Visa-free access for nationals listed in Annex II is for max 90 days within any 180-day period for non-employment purposes. Airport transit visas (Category A) are required for nationals of Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Democratic Republic of Congo, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Ghana, Iran, Iraq, Nigeria, Pakistan, Somalia, Sri Lanka, and Syria. ETIAS pre-travel authorization is a forthcoming requirement for visa-exempt third-country nationals, targeted for Q4 2026. Croatia joined the EU and Schengen Area in 2023 and is covered under EU Member States, not as a third-country entry. Kosovo nationals with biometric passports have been visa-free since 1 January 2024.

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid Schengen uniform visa (Visum C) is exempt from the airport transit visa (Category A) requirement. Applies to nationals of countries that otherwise require a transit visa (Afghanistan, Bangladesh, DRC, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Ghana, Iran, Iraq, Nigeria, Pakistan, Somalia, Sri Lanka, Syria). Does not grant entry into Austria beyond the transit zone; only exempts from the Category A transit visa requirement.

Holder of a valid national long-stay visa (Visum D) issued by any Schengen member state is exempt from the airport transit visa (Category A) requirement. Applies to nationals of countries that otherwise require a transit visa. Does not grant entry into Austria beyond the transit zone; only exempts from the Category A transit visa requirement.

Holder of a valid residence permit (Aufenthaltstitel) issued by any Schengen member state is exempt from the airport transit visa (Category A) requirement. Applies to nationals of countries that otherwise require a transit visa. Does not grant entry into Austria beyond the transit zone; only exempts from the Category A transit visa requirement.

Holder of a valid residence permit issued by Ireland is exempt from the airport transit visa (Category A) requirement. Applies to nationals of countries that otherwise require a transit visa. Does not grant entry into Austria beyond the transit zone; only exempts from the Category A transit visa requirement.

Holder of a valid residence permit issued by Cyprus is exempt from the airport transit visa (Category A) requirement. Applies to nationals of countries that otherwise require a transit visa. Does not grant entry into Austria beyond the transit zone; only exempts from the Category A transit visa requirement.

credential.subtype: any valid residence permit guaranteeing unconditional readmission

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid residence permit from Andorra that guarantees unconditional readmission of the holder (vorbehaltlose Rückübernahme) is exempt from the airport transit visa (Category A) requirement. Applies to nationals of countries that otherwise require a transit visa.

credential.subtype: any valid residence permit guaranteeing unconditional readmission

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid residence permit from Canada that guarantees unconditional readmission of the holder (vorbehaltlose Rückübernahme) is exempt from the airport transit visa (Category A) requirement. Applies to nationals of countries that otherwise require a transit visa.

credential.subtype: any valid residence permit guaranteeing unconditional readmission

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid residence permit from Japan that guarantees unconditional readmission of the holder (vorbehaltlose Rückübernahme) is exempt from the airport transit visa (Category A) requirement. Applies to nationals of countries that otherwise require a transit visa.

credential.subtype: any valid residence permit guaranteeing unconditional readmission

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid residence permit from San Marino that guarantees unconditional readmission of the holder (vorbehaltlose Rückübernahme) is exempt from the airport transit visa (Category A) requirement. Applies to nationals of countries that otherwise require a transit visa.

credential.subtype: any valid UK residence permit guaranteeing unconditional readmission

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid residence permit from the United Kingdom that guarantees unconditional readmission of the holder (vorbehaltlose Rückübernahme) is exempt from the airport transit visa (Category A) requirement. Applies to nationals of countries that otherwise require a transit visa.

credential.subtype: any valid US residence permit guaranteeing unconditional readmission

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid residence permit from the United States that guarantees unconditional readmission of the holder (vorbehaltlose Rückübernahme) is exempt from the airport transit visa (Category A) requirement. Applies to nationals of countries that otherwise require a transit visa.

credential.subtype: any valid Irish visa (when travelling to/from Ireland)

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid Irish visa is exempt from the airport transit visa (Category A) requirement when travelling to or returning from Ireland. Applies to nationals of countries that otherwise require a transit visa.

credential.subtype: any valid Bulgarian visa (when travelling to/from Bulgaria)

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid Bulgarian visa is exempt from the airport transit visa (Category A) requirement when travelling to or returning from Bulgaria. Applies to nationals of countries that otherwise require a transit visa.

credential.subtype: any valid Romanian visa (when travelling to/from Romania)

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid Romanian visa is exempt from the airport transit visa (Category A) requirement when travelling to or returning from Romania. Applies to nationals of countries that otherwise require a transit visa.

credential.subtype: any valid Cypriot visa (when travelling to/from Cyprus)

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid Cypriot visa is exempt from the airport transit visa (Category A) requirement when travelling to or returning from Cyprus. Applies to nationals of countries that otherwise require a transit visa.

credential.subtype: any valid Croatian visa (when travelling to/from Croatia)

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid Croatian visa is exempt from the airport transit visa (Category A) requirement when travelling to or returning from Croatia. Note: Croatia joined Schengen in 2024, so this provision may now be moot for Croatian visas issued after Schengen accession. Applies to nationals of countries that otherwise require a transit visa.

credential.subtype: any valid Canadian visa (when travelling to/from Canada)

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid Canadian visa is exempt from the airport transit visa (Category A) requirement when travelling to or returning from Canada. Applies to nationals of countries that otherwise require a transit visa.

credential.subtype: any valid Japanese visa (when travelling to/from Japan)

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid Japanese visa is exempt from the airport transit visa (Category A) requirement when travelling to or returning from Japan. Applies to nationals of countries that otherwise require a transit visa.

credential.subtype: any valid US visa (when travelling to/from USA)

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid US visa is exempt from the airport transit visa (Category A) requirement when travelling to or returning from the USA. Applies to nationals of countries that otherwise require a transit visa.

Bilateral visa suppression agreement (Visumbefreiungsabkommen). Egyptian holders of diplomatic, service, or special passports may enter Austria visa-free for up to 90 days within any 180-day period. BGBl. Nr. 19/2014.
Bilateral visa suppression agreement. Azerbaijani holders of diplomatic and service passports may enter Austria visa-free. BGBl. Nr. 145/2010.
Bilateral visa suppression agreement. Bahamian holders of diplomatic and service passports may enter Austria visa-free for up to 3 months. BGBl. Nr. 23/1982, amended by BGBl. III Nr. 126/1998.
Bilateral visa suppression agreement. Barbadian holders of diplomatic and service passports may enter Austria visa-free for up to 3 months. BGBl. Nr. 248/1973, amended by BGBl. III Nr. 215/1997.
Bilateral visa suppression agreement. Belizean holders of diplomatic passports only (not service passports) may enter Austria visa-free for up to 90 days. BGBl. Nr. 205/2002.
Bilateral visa suppression agreement. Bolivian holders of diplomatic and service passports may enter Austria visa-free for up to 90 days. BGBl. Nr. 216/1960, amended by BGBl. Nr. 47/2007.
Bilateral visa suppression agreement. Ivorian holders of diplomatic and service passports may enter Austria visa-free for up to 90 days. BGBl. Nr. 298/1985.
Bilateral visa suppression agreement. Ecuadorian holders of diplomatic, service, and special passports may enter Austria visa-free for up to 90 days. BGBl. Nr. 79/2003.

Bilateral visa suppression agreement covering all passport categories. El Salvadoran holders of all passport types may enter Austria visa-free for up to 90 days. (Ordinary passport holders are also visa-free under EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II.)
Bilateral visa suppression agreement. Indian holders of diplomatic passports only (not service passports) may enter Austria visa-free for up to 90 days. BGBl. Nr. 126/2023.
Bilateral visa suppression agreement. Indonesian holders of diplomatic and service passports may enter Austria visa-free for up to 90 days. BGBl. Nr. 146/2008.

Bilateral visa-free travel agreement for holders of diplomatic passports, signed 7 May 2026 in Tashkent by Austrian Foreign Minister Beate Meinl-Reisinger and Interior Minister Gerhard Karner. No BGBl reference published as of signing date.

cbi.program_name: Austrian Citizenship for Exceptional Services (Staatsbürgerschaftsgesetz §10 Abs. 6)

options.notes: No fixed investment threshold is set in the law. The provision requires 'extraordinary services' (außerordentliche Leistungen) in the special interest of the Republic, which may include economic, scientific, cultural or athletic contributions. The Federal Government (Council of Ministers) confirms each case individually. There is no published minimum investment figure on the official source; non-official sources report figures of €3 million–€10 million but these are NOT confirmed on official government pages and are not recorded here.

cbi.processing_time: Not specified on official source. Council of Ministers meets twice yearly to decide applications.
cbi.notes: Section 10(6) of the Austrian Citizenship Act (Staatsbürgerschaftsgesetz) allows the Federal Government to grant citizenship without satisfying the standard requirements (10 years residency, secured livelihood, renunciation of foreign citizenship) when the grant is in the special interest of the Republic based on extraordinary services already rendered and expected to continue. This is a discretionary merit-based program, not a conventional CBI program with a published investment schedule. Applications are filed at one of Austria's 9 provincial governments. Dual citizenship is permitted under this provision. Official source text (German): 'Die Voraussetzungen des Abs. 1 Z 1 und 7 sowie des Abs. 3 entfallen, wenn die Bundesregierung bestätigt, daß die Verleihung der Staatsbürgerschaft wegen der vom Fremden bereits erbrachten und von ihm noch zu erwartenden außerordentlichen Leistungen im besonderen Interesse der Republik liegt.'

programs.name: Red-White-Red Card – Very Highly Qualified Workers
programs.notes: Points-based system; minimum 70 points required. Key criteria: tertiary education (up to 40 pts), work experience (up to 20 pts), language skills (up to 10 pts), age under 35–45 (up to 20 pts), Austrian studies (up to 10 pts). Card valid 24 months, employer-tied. After 24 months with min. 21 months employment, eligible for Red-White-Red Card Plus (unrestricted labor market access). Standard citizenship requires 10 years legal residence (5 with residence permit).

programs.name: Red-White-Red Card – Skilled Workers in Shortage Occupations
programs.notes: For skilled workers in occupations on the Austrian shortage occupation list. Points-based assessment. Card valid 24 months, employer-tied. Eligible for Red-White-Red Card Plus after 24 months.

programs.name: Red-White-Red Card – Self-Employed Key Workers
programs.notes: Self-employed must demonstrate macroeconomic benefit through at least one of: minimum €100,000 capital transfer to Austria, job creation/preservation, knowledge/technology transfer, or regional economic significance. No points system applies. Processing of macroeconomic benefit assessment by Public Employment Service (AMS) within 3 weeks. Card valid 24 months. Minimum amount verified against official source.

programs.name: Red-White-Red Card – Start-up Founders
programs.notes: Requires founding a company developing innovative products/services/technologies, a coherent business plan, controlling influence (min. 50% equity), and minimum €30,000 capital. Minimum 50 points required. Points system: Qualification max 30 pts (vocational 20, tertiary 20, Austrian degree 30), Work Experience max 10 pts (1 pt per half-year), Language max 15 pts (German A2=5, B1-B2=10, C1-C2=15; English B2=10), Bonus max 30 pts (€50k+ extra capital=10, incubator=10, age ≤35=10). AMS reviews within 3 weeks. Card valid up to 24 months. Points system updated: maximum is 85 pts total (not previously specified).

programs.name: EU Blue Card Austria
programs.notes: For third-country nationals with university degree (min. 3 years) and job offer for min. 6 months. Salary must equal at least the average gross annual salary for full-time employees. Card issued for 24 months. After 21 of 24 months in employment, eligible for Red-White-Red Card Plus.

programs.name: Red-White-Red Card Plus
programs.notes: Grants unrestricted labor market access and fixed-term settlement. Available to: holders of RWR Card after successful first period, EU Blue Card holders after 21 months employment, family members of RWR Card holders. Leads eventually to long-term EU settlement permit and standard naturalization after 10 years legal residence.

programs.name: Red-White-Red Card – Very Highly Qualified Workers
programs.eligibility: Minimum 70 points on the Austrian points system. Requires tertiary education degree, work experience, language skills. Must secure a qualifying job offer.
programs.processing_time: AMS assessment within 3 weeks; overall processing time not specified on official source.
programs.notes: Points-based: 40 pts for qualifications (PhD/habilitation maximum), 20 pts for work experience, 10 pts for language, 20 pts for age, 10 pts for Austrian studies. Minimum threshold: 70 points.

programs.name: EU Blue Card Austria
programs.eligibility: University degree (min. 3 years), binding job offer for min. 6 months, salary at least equal to average gross annual salary for full-time employees, labor market test.
programs.processing_time: Not specified on official source; one-stop-shop procedure with AMS.
programs.notes: EU-wide instrument, implemented in Austria. Card issued for 24 months.

programs.name: Red-White-Red Card – Start-up Founders
programs.eligibility: Innovative business, coherent business plan, min. 50% equity, min. €30,000 capital, min. 50 points on points system.
programs.processing_time: AMS macroeconomic benefit review within 3 weeks.
programs.notes: Min. €30,000 company capital required with 50% equity stake. Extra 10 points for additional €50,000 capital. Card valid up to 24 months. Maximum 85 points total across all categories.

programs.name: Working Holiday Programme
programs.eligibility: Age 18–30 years. Available to nationals of: Argentina, Australia, Canada, Chile, Hong Kong, India, Israel, Japan, New Zealand, South Korea, Taiwan, United States of America.
programs.notes: Bilateral Working Holiday Programme agreements with 12 countries. Primary purpose is holiday with incidental work/training permitted. Not a permanent residence or fast-track-to-citizenship pathway.

validation_notes: ADDED Kosovo (XKX): visa-free since 1 January 2024 for biometric passport holders under EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II (as amended). Was entirely absent from prior record.
ADDED United Arab Emirates (ARE): visa-free under EU-UAE short-stay visa waiver agreement and Annex II of EU Regulation 2018/1806. Was entirely absent from prior record.

ADDED Kiribati (KIR), Marshall Islands (MHL), Micronesia/FSM (FSM), Nauru (NRU), San Marino (SMR), Solomon Islands (SLB), Timor-Leste (TLS), Tuvalu (TUV): all listed in Annex II of EU Regulation 2018/1806 but omitted from prior record as they were not enumerated on the Austrian Embassy London page.

ADDED British Nationals (Overseas) and other British nationality categories: Annex II lists five distinct British nationality categories beyond British Citizenship. Prior record only covered 'British Citizenship'.

CORRECTED Croatia entry: Prior record listed Croatia as a separate third-country visa-free entry. Croatia joined the EU and became a full Schengen member. It is now covered under 'EU Member States (all)' and should not appear as a separate third-country entry. Removed the erroneous standalone Croatia entry.

CORRECTED ETIAS status note: Prior record described ETIAS as 'announced but not yet fully operational'. Updated to reflect current status: ETIAS is NOT YET operational as of June 2026; EU official timeline targets Q4 2026 launch.
UPDATED RBI start-up founders points breakdown: confirmed maximum is 85 points total (Qualification 30, Work Experience 10, Language 15, Bonus 30); prior record did not specify the maximum total.
UPDATED source_urls for Annex II countries: Added EUR-Lex consolidated Regulation 2018/1806 URL as authoritative source for newly added Annex II countries.
RETAINED Venezuela (VEN): No formal suspension of visa waiver enacted as of June 2026 despite ongoing EU discussions under the visa suspension mechanism.
RETAINED all conditional_access entries: Verified against BMI transit visa page; airport transit visa exemption structure unchanged.
gaps: ETIAS exact launch date within Q4 2026 not confirmed; EU has not published a specific date as of June 2026.

The CBI provision (§10 Abs. 6 Staatsbürgerschaftsgesetz) has no published minimum investment amount on any official government source. Non-official sources cite €3–10 million figures but these cannot be verified and are omitted.

EU Blue Card minimum salary figure (exact annual EUR amount) not specified on official bmi.gv.at source page; stated as 'at least the average gross annual salary for full-time employees' without quoting the current figure.
No dedicated digital nomad visa exists in Austria as of sources checked; freelancers may use the self-employed key worker route.

The BMI visa requirements page (bmi.gv.at) bilateral diplomatic/service passport agreements may be incomplete; the page content truncates after Indonesia alphabetically in all fetch attempts. Additional countries with passport-type-only waivers beyond Indonesia (names starting J–Z, excluding Uzbekistan confirmed separately) cannot be confirmed from the live page.

Venezuela visa-free status requires monitoring: EU Parliament raised questions about applying the suspension mechanism to Venezuela in 2025; no suspension enacted as of June 2026 but situation is subject to change.

Croatia's conditional_access entry for airport transit visa exemption (based on holding a Croatian visa) may be moot post-Schengen accession — Croatian visas issued post-2024 are Schengen visas and would already be covered by the Schengen visa exemption. Retained for completeness pending official BMI page update.

outbound_official.notes: Austria's BMEIA (Bundesministerium für europäische und internationale Angelegenheiten) publishes per-country travel information pages at bmeia.gv.at/reise-services/reiseinformation/land/[country] for Austrian citizens traveling abroad. Each page contains an 'Einreise & Ausreise' (Entry & Exit) section stating the Visumpflicht (visa requirement: yes/no) and permitted stay. There is no single consolidated visa-free list; data below is compiled from individual country pages. Austrian citizens as EU/Schengen nationals have free movement within all EU and EEA states plus Switzerland — those are not enumerated separately below. All EU/EEA/CH destinations are implicitly visa-free with no stay limit. Canada requires eTA only for air travel; entry by land/sea is visa-free without eTA. South Korea eTA (K-ETA) exemption for Austrians is in effect until 31 Dec 2026. China visa-free 30 days valid until 31 Dec 2026.

visa_free.name: Brazil
visa_free.max_stay: not specified (sometimes limited to 30 days at border)

visa_on_arrival.name: Indonesia
visa_on_arrival.notes: Paid VOA; also available as e-visa online

visa_on_arrival.name: Jordan
visa_on_arrival.notes: VOA at airport JOD 40; e-visa recommended in advance

visa_on_arrival.name: Madagascar
visa_on_arrival.notes: VOA at international airports and seaports

visa_on_arrival.name: Namibia

visa_on_arrival.name: Nepal

visa_on_arrival.name: Qatar
visa_on_arrival.max_stay: 180 days single-entry or 90 days multi-entry per 180 days

visa_on_arrival.name: Zimbabwe
visa_on_arrival.notes: Also available as e-visa; e-visa not always accepted at land borders

e_visa.name: Australia
e_visa.max_stay: multiple entries up to 90 days each
e_visa.notes: eVisitor (subclass 651); must apply online before travel; no fee for tourists

e_visa.name: Azerbaijan
e_visa.notes: Standard e-visa (3 business days) or urgent (3 hours)

e_visa.name: Bahrain
e_visa.notes: E-visa at evisa.gov.bh or visa on arrival

e_visa.name: Cuba
e_visa.notes: E-visa at evisacuba.cu; registration at dviajeros.mitrans.gob.cu required 7 days before

e_visa.name: India
e_visa.notes: E-visa for specific entry purposes via designated airports/seaports

e_visa.name: Laos
e_visa.notes: E-visa at laoevisa.gov.la; visa on arrival at select crossings

e_visa.name: Mozambique
e_visa.notes: E-visa system noted as technically unreliable

e_visa.name: Saudi Arabia
e_visa.notes: Tourist e-visa at visa.visitsaudi.com; also visa on arrival with credit card

e_visa.name: Sri Lanka
e_visa.notes: ETA at eta.gov.lk; must obtain before arrival

e_visa.name: Vietnam
e_visa.notes: E-visa at evisa.gov.vn; must be printed

e_visa.name: Cameroon
e_visa.notes: E-visa at evisacam.cm; EUR 167; 4–5 business days processing

e_visa.name: Myanmar
e_visa.notes: Level 4 travel warning; visa details not fully confirmed on BMEIA page

eta.name: Canada
eta.notes: eTA required for air and transit travel; NOT required for entry by land/sea; valid 5 years or until passport expiry

eta.name: Kenya
eta.notes: Electronic Travel Authorisation (eTA) mandatory before arrival; printed copy required

eta.name: United States
eta.notes: ESTA required (Visa Waiver Program); apply min. 72 hours before departure; valid 2 years
